document.title: Clinical Comparative Study with a Large-Area Amorphous Silicon Flat-Panel Detector: Image Quality and Visibility of Anatomic Structures on Chest Radiography

sourceSummary.oneLine: AJR clinical comparative study evaluating large-area amorphous silicon flat-panel digital chest radiography against conventional film-screen radiography.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Clinical literature evidence for validation and verification of digital flat-panel X-ray detector image quality, anatomic visibility, and dose reduction.
keyPoints: Routine follow-up chest radiographs from 100 consecutive oncology patients were compared between a flat-panel digital radiography system and a conventional film-screen system.
Phantom dose measurements showed approximately 50% dose reduction with the digital radiography system compared with the film-screen system.
Image quality and visibility of all but one evaluated anatomic structure were rated significantly superior for the digital flat-panel detector system.
The authors concluded that flat-panel detector images were perceived as equal or superior despite approximately 50% reduced radiation dose.

entities.name: Revolution XQ/i
entities.evidence: The digital radiography system (Revolution XQ/i; General Electric Medical Systems, Milwaukee, WI) consisted of an X-ray tube, a generator, and a large-area amorphous silicon flat-panel detector.

entities.name: General Electric Medical Systems
entities.evidence: Revolution XQ/i; General Electric Medical Systems, Milwaukee, WI

entities.name: Maxiray 100
entities.evidence: The digital radiography system also included a standard X-ray tube (Maxiray 100; General Electric Medical Systems).

entities.name: University of Heidelberg
entities.evidence: Department of Diagnostic Radiology, University of Heidelberg, INF 110, 69120 Heidelberg, Germany.

concepts.name: Large-area amorphous silicon flat-panel detector
concepts.evidence: The panel consists of a cesium iodide scintillator and an amorphous silicon photodiode array fabricated on a glass substrate.

concepts.name: Digital chest radiography
concepts.evidence: The objective of this study was to compare clinical chest radiographs of a large-area, flat-panel digital radiography system and a conventional film-screen radiography system.

concepts.name: Dose reduction
concepts.pageTarget: Radiation Dose Reduction In Digital Radiography
concepts.evidence: Dose measurements with a chest phantom showed a dose reduction of approximately 50% with the digital radiography system compared with the film-screen radiography system.

concepts.name: Detective quantum efficiency
concepts.evidence: Detective quantum efficiency is generally accepted as the most important parameter for characterizing the performance of a detector system.

concepts.name: Observer preference study
concepts.evidence: The comparison was based on an observer preference study of image quality and visibility of anatomic structures.

evidence.claim: The article was published in AJR volume 178 in February 2002.
The study compared a large-area flat-panel digital radiography system with a conventional film-screen radiography system for chest radiographs.
evidence.evidenceText: The objective of this study was to compare clinical chest radiographs of a large-area, flat-panel digital radiography system and a conventional film-screen radiography system.

evidence.claim: The clinical comparison used routine follow-up chest radiographs from 100 consecutive oncology patients.
evidence.evidenceText: Routine follow-up chest radiographs were obtained from 100 consecutive oncology patients using a large-area, amorphous silicon flat-panel detector digital radiography system.

evidence.claim: The digital radiography system identified in the study was Revolution XQ/i from General Electric Medical Systems.
evidence.evidenceText: The digital radiography system (Revolution XQ/i; General Electric Medical Systems, Milwaukee, WI) consisted of an X-ray tube, a generator, and a large-area amorphous silicon flat-panel detector.

evidence.claim: The detector used cesium iodide scintillator and an amorphous silicon photodiode array.
evidence.evidenceText: The panel consists of a cesium iodide scintillator and an amorphous silicon photodiode array fabricated on a glass substrate.

evidence.claim: The detector matrix was 2048 × 2048 pixels with 200 μm pixel size and a 41 × 41 cm active area.
evidence.evidenceText: The detector has a matrix size of 2048 × 2048 pixels, with a pixel size of 200 μm. The active area of the detector measures 41 × 41 cm.

evidence.claim: Phantom dose measurements showed a 50.1% dose reduction with the digital system compared with film-screen radiography.
evidence.evidenceText: The dose measurements performed with the anthropomorphic chest phantom before the patient examinations showed a dose reduction of 50.1% with the digital system compared with the conventional film-screen system.
evidence.pageTarget: Radiation Dose Reduction In Digital Radiography

evidence.claim: Patient dose measurements were not performed.
evidence.evidenceText: Dose measurements for the patient examinations were not performed.

evidence.claim: Image quality for digital flat-panel detector radiographs was rated significantly superior to conventional film-screen radiographs.
evidence.evidenceText: The quality of the images obtained with the digital flat-panel detector system was rated significantly superior to the quality of those obtained with the conventional film-screen radiography system.

evidence.claim: Visibility of all but one evaluated anatomic structure was rated significantly superior with the digital system.
evidence.evidenceText: The visibility of all but one anatomic structure on the images obtained with the digital system was rated significantly superior to the images obtained with the reference film-screen system.
evidence.pageTarget: Anatomic Structure Visibility In Chest Radiography

evidence.claim: The authors concluded that flat-panel detector image quality and structure visibility were equal or superior despite approximately 50% lower radiation dose.
evidence.evidenceText: The image quality and the visibility of structures on the images obtained with a flat-panel detector based on amorphous silicon were perceived as equal or superior to images obtained with a conventional film-screen radiography system.

reviewFlags.reason: No regulatory authority or jurisdiction is stated in the source text; authoritySignal indicates FDA, but the document itself is an AJR literature article.
No revision or version number is present in the source text.
No standards are cited as applicable standards in the source text.
The source is clinical literature supporting validation/verification rather than an official regulatory approval document.
